intro: Murucuja herbertiana, also known as Kava or Piper methysticum, is a plant species native to the Pacific Island region, belonging to the pepper family (Piperaceae). The health benefits of Murucuja herbertiana include its anxiolytic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and pain. The bioactive constituents of this plant include kavalactones, such as kawain, desmethoxymethysticin, and yangonin, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Murucuja herbertiana is commonly used in herbal preparations such as kava tea, kava capsules, and topical creams, which are used to alleviate anxiety, insomnia, and muscle tension.
